domain_terms.BPM Platform: Process orchestration / workflow engine that executes BPMN 2.0 or CMMN models with persistent state, human tasks, and process variables. Examples: Camunda, IBM BAW, Activiti, jBPM, Bonita, Flowable, Pega, Appian, ProcessMaker, Oracle BPM Suite, TIBCO ActiveMatrix BPM
domain_terms.Document Management System (DMS): Enterprise content management system that stores, classifies, versions, and applies retention policies to documents with an API or workflow surface. Examples: IBM FileNet (P8), Athento, SharePoint (when used as DMS), OpenText Documentum, Alfresco, OpenText Content Server, Nuxeo, Hyland OnBase, Box Enterprise, M-Files
domain_terms.BPMN (Business Process Model and Notatio: ISO/IEC 19510 graphical notation for executable process diagrams; the modeling language most BPM platforms execute natively
domain_terms.CMMN (Case Management Model and Notation: OMG standard for case management; alternative to BPMN for unstructured / knowledge-worker processes
domain_terms.Process Instance: A single in-flight execution of a BPMN process model — used for cycle-time and throughput measurements
domain_terms.Object Store / Repository: Logical container for documents and their metadata in a DMS (FileNet Object Store, Athento dossier, SharePoint Site Collection, Alfresco repository)
domain_terms.Document Class / Content Type: Schema definition for a category of documents (e.g., 'Loan Application', 'Disclosure', 'ID Document'); carries property definitions and lifecycle hooks
domain_terms.Records Management / Retention Policy: Rule set governing how long documents are retained, when they are eligible for disposition, and the audit trail required during their lifecycle
domain_terms.CMIS (Content Management Interoperabilit: OASIS standard REST/SOAP API for cross-DMS interoperability; supported natively by FileNet, Alfresco, SharePoint, Nuxeo
domain_terms.Federation / Connection license: DMS license that authorizes the platform to read or write a separate repository (e.g., FileNet federation to a legacy archive)

abbreviations.LAA: Process Transformation compliance requirement code (BPM / DMS scoped)
abbreviations.BAW: IBM Business Automation Workflow (formerly IBM BPM)

primary_source_sections: 5 (Component Model — BPMN/CMMN diagrams + DMS components), 6 (Data Model — DMS object model, Document Classes, retention), 7 (Integration View — BPM connectors + DMS APIs), 8 (Technology Stack — BPM/DMS platform names + license metrics), 10 (Non-Functional Requirements — throughput + sizing), 11 (Operational Considerations — execution model + retention + monitoring)
framework_description: BPM platforms (Camunda, IBM BAW, Pega, Appian, Activiti, Flowable, Bonita, ProcessMaker, Oracle BPM, TIBCO ActiveMatrix BPM) and Document Management Systems (FileNet, Athento, SharePoint, Documentum, Alfresco, OpenText Content Server, Nuxeo, OnBase, Box Governance, M-Files) — including process orchestration, document lifecycle management, retention/records management, and platform licensing alignment. Contract is Not Applicable when neither a BPM platform nor a DMS is in the architecture.
